notes: Data-lead: specific number of filings across 20 clients = 160+ deadlines. System break point is precise.
title: Managing MOL filing deadlines across 20 client companies — when exactly does the system break?
brief: Data-lead: specific number of filings across 20 clients = 160+ deadlines. System break point is precise.
body:
20 client companies. 8 MOL filings per company per year. That's 160 government deadlines.

One Sanad office. One PRO officer. One shared spreadsheet.

I want you to do the math on that before you keep reading.

160 deadlines across 20 clients means on average one filing is due every 2.3 days. Some months it clusters — Ramadan renewals, new hire seasons, corporate restructuring. In those months, a single PRO officer is managing 12–15 active submissions simultaneously.

And the spreadsheet doesn't know that two of them are due on the same day. It just sits there.

Here's exactly when the system breaks:

The first miss is always small — a filing submitted 3 days late because the officer thought it was next week. A OMR 50 fine. Client is understanding.

The second miss is in a different client's file. The officer has 8 active cases that week. They didn't see the date slip. OMR 150 fine.

By the third miss, the client has already started asking around.

The issue isn't the officer's capability. It's that the tool they're using was designed to store data, not to manage deadlines across 20 simultaneous client relationships.

SmartPRO Hub tracks every MOL filing deadline per client, sends automatic alerts 30 days and 7 days out, and shows the PRO officer their full weekly workload in one view — not across 20 tabs.

160 deadlines. One dashboard.

How many MOL filing deadlines are you tracking right now — and what happens when one slips?
